description: Updating balances or flags after an external call, letting a malicious callback re-enter and withdraw repeatedly.
body: Failure mode: a withdrawal sends ETH or calls a token before decrementing the user's balance, so the recipient's fallback re-enters the function while the balance still reads its original value and drains funds across nested calls. Detection signals: a state write (balance decrement, flag set) appears after an external call; an adversarial reentrancy test succeeds in extracting more than deposited; no reentrancy guard. Do not perform external calls before finalizing state. Safer fallback: reorder to checks-effects-interactions so all state updates precede the external call, add a reentrancy guard for defense in depth, and verify with a malicious re-entering mock that the exploit now fails.
